findings.title: No evaluation/inference pipeline, weights, or data — no paper number is reproducible
findings.claim: The repo has only a Stage-1 and Stage-2 training entrypoint (basicsr/train.py). There is no basicsr/test.py or any inference/metric-computing script, no requirements file, no pretrained weights (*.pth/*.ckpt), and no benchmark data; the README is a single title line. Default configs set val.metrics: ~ so even validation computes no metrics.
findings.concern: Every PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/FID/FLOPs/runtime value in Tables 1–5 and every Fig. 2 motivation curve is untraceable: nothing in the repo computes the reported numbers, and the trained models and benchmark datasets needed to do so are absent.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the evaluation/inference scripts that produce each table, the trained checkpoints, the dataset access (or fetch scripts), and a requirements/environment file with the exact commands per table.
findings.reason: README.md is confirmed to contain only the single title line '# Latent-Harmony'; no test.py, requirements.txt, setup.py, or environment.yml exists anywhere in the repo.

findings.title: Paper's latent restoration network R_theta and L_Res step are absent from Stage 2
findings.claim: The Stage-2 network RAVAEHFLora wraps only the VAE (self.vae) plus LoRA adapters; forward() just runs the VAE on the degraded input. There is no latent restoration network R_theta, and the Stage-2 trainer (VAEadapter_model.py) never trains an R_theta nor uses a main restoration loss L_Res — its only losses are the HF fidelity L1 and the HF-GAN loss.
findings.concern: The paper's entire Stage 2 (Eq. 7: z_res = R_theta(z_deg), L_Res = ||D_psi*(z_res) - I_clean||_1, trained first with a frozen VAE, then HF-LoRA refinement) is the core of the method and the source of the restoration results; the code implements only a VAE that maps the degraded image to itself through LoRA-adapted encoder/decoder, so the released code cannot reproduce the restoration described.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the R_theta restoration-network definition, the L_Res pre-training step, and confirm how z_res enters the decoder; clarify whether the released Stage-2 code is complete.
findings.reason: Lines 122-133 of LHVAE_hflora_arch.py exactly match the quoted forward() method, which only invokes self.vae with no R_theta restoration network present.

findings.title: No requirements/environment file though many third-party deps are imported
findings.claim: The repo imports torch, torchvision, pyiqa, einops, cv2, numpy, scipy, PIL, tqdm and yaml at module load, but contains no requirements.txt, setup.py, environment.yml or any other dependency specification (confirmed by check_completeness.py).
findings.concern: The environment cannot be rebuilt deterministically; in particular unpinned pyiqa/torch/torchvision versions affect the LPIPS/metric values the paper reports.
findings.resolution: Authors: add a pinned requirements.txt or environment.yml listing all dependencies and versions.
findings.reason: VAEadapter_model.py line 8 is confirmed to be 'import pyiqa'; a repo-wide search finds no requirements.txt, setup.py, or environment.yml.

findings.title: Key architecture modules are placeholder/substitute stubs, not the paper's components
findings.quote:
class VGGFeatureExtractor(nn.Module):
    """Fallback VGG feature extractor placeholder.

    Returns a dict with requested layer names, each mapped to the input itself.
    """
findings.claim: Several components are explicitly self-described as stand-ins: VGGFeatureExtractor 'returns the input itself' (vgg_arch.py:4-8); restormer.TransformerBlock is a 'Lightweight spatial transformer-style block' (restormer.py:5); fourmer.ProcessBlock is a 'Lightweight substitute for Fourmer process block' (fourmer.py:5). The paper states the latent restoration network is SFHformer, which does not appear here.
findings.concern: If the perceptual/feature and restoration components are placeholders rather than the real modules, the repo is not the artefact that produced the paper's numbers, and the reported results (which depend on these exact modules) cannot be reproduced from it.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether these are placeholders, and release the actual SFHformer restoration network and the real VGG/perceptual modules used for the reported metrics.
findings.reason: vgg_arch.py lines 4-8 exactly match the quoted placeholder docstring; the forward() method confirms it returns the input unchanged instead of VGG features.

findings.title: Stage-2 config requests unregistered arch 'RAVAE' — network build raises KeyError
findings.claim: configs/stage2_hflora.yml sets vae_config.type: RAVAE, and RAVAEHFLora.__init__ does ARCH_REGISTRY.get(arch_type)(**cfg) (LHVAE_hflora_arch.py:38). The only arch names registered by the auto-imported *_arch.py modules are RAVAE_EQ, RAVAEHFLora, UNetDiscriminatorSN and LoRAConv2d (verified by AST scan); 'RAVAE' is never registered, so ARCH_REGISTRY.get('RAVAE') raises KeyError.
findings.concern: The Stage-2 training config cannot instantiate its network as written; Stage-2 training crashes at startup, so the provided config does not run.
findings.resolution: Authors: change vae_config.type to RAVAE_EQ (the registered class) or register a class named RAVAE, and confirm which VAE the Stage-2 results used.
findings.reason: stage2_hflora.yml line 42 is confirmed to request 'type: RAVAE'; the only registered arch classes are RAVAE_EQ (LHVAE_arch.py) and RAVAEHFLora (LHVAE_hflora_arch.py); 'RAVAE' is absent, and LHVAE_hflora_arch.py:38 confirms ARCH_REGISTRY.get(arch_type) is called directly.

findings.title: Hardcoded author-machine absolute paths break runs on any other system
findings.claim: options.py:159 hardcodes the experiments root to '/fs-computility/ai4sData/liuyidi/model/...'. Additional hardcoded sys.path.append paths exist: train.py:8 and LHVAE_arch.py:7 ('/fs-computility/ai4sData/liuyidi/code/LatentGen'), attention.py:8 ('/code/UHDFour_code-main'), encoder_3.py:8 ('/code/UHDformer-main').
findings.concern: Training writes checkpoints/logs to an absolute path that does not exist on a reviewer's machine, and the sys.path.append lines point at directories not in the repo; the code is not portable as released.
findings.resolution: Authors: make experiments_root relative to root_path (as the test branch already does) and remove the absolute sys.path.append calls.
findings.reason: options.py lines 158-160 exactly match the quoted code block with the hardcoded '/fs-computility/ai4sData/liuyidi/model' path.

findings.title: Decoder/PHF-LoRA step also applies HF fidelity L1, not the perception-only loss of Eq. 9
findings.quote:
recon, posterior, z = self._forward_g()
hf_pred = self._hf(recon)

l_total = recon.new_tensor(0.0)
l_hf_fid = self.cri_pix(hf_pred, hf_gt) * self.lambda_hf_fid
l_total = l_total + l_hf_fid
loss_dict['l_hf_fid'] = l_hf_fid

if self.net_d_hf is not None and self.cri_gan is not None and self.lambda_gan > 0:
    pred_fake_g = self.net_d_hf(hf_pred)
    l_hf_gan = self.cri_gan(pred_fake_g, True, is_disc=False) * self.lambda_gan
    l_total = l_total + l_hf_gan
findings.claim: In the PHF-LoRA (decoder) optimization step the loss is l_hf_fid (HF fidelity L1, the same loss used for the encoder FHF-LoRA step) plus l_hf_gan; the decoder LoRA is updated by both.
findings.concern: The paper states PHF-LoRA is guided by a perception-oriented high-frequency loss implemented as a GAN loss (Eq. 9) only, decoupled from the fidelity loss; including the fidelity L1 in the decoder step contradicts the described 'differentiated mechanisms' and the perception-vs-fidelity decoupling that motivates the α control.
findings.resolution: Authors: clarify whether the decoder LoRA was trained with the fidelity L1 in addition to the GAN loss, and reconcile with Eq. 9 / Section 3.3.
findings.reason: VAEadapter_model.py lines 190-202 exactly match the quoted code; the decoder (PHF-LoRA) step unconditionally adds l_hf_fid before optionally adding l_hf_gan.

findings.title: Stage-1 reconstruction encodes the perturbed image, not I_clean as in Eq. 2
findings.quote:
self.pdps_input, t_value, pdps_branch = self._build_pdps_input(current_iter)
raw_out = self.net_g(self.pdps_input)
findings.claim: Stage-1 feeds the PDPS-perturbed/degraded image (pdps_input) through the VAE and computes the reconstruction loss l_recon = cri_pix(recon, gt) against the clean image (line 318).
findings.concern: Eq. 2 writes L_VAE = ||D_psi(E_phi(I_clean)) - I_clean||_1 (encode the CLEAN image); the code reconstructs the clean target from the degraded input. This is a valid restoration-style objective but differs from the equation as written, so the paper's formula does not match the implemented loss.
findings.resolution: Authors: update Eq. 2 to reflect that the encoder input is the PDPS-perturbed image, or confirm which input the reported Stage-1 used.
findings.reason: EQvae_model.py lines 298-299 exactly match the quote; net_g receives pdps_input (perturbed), and line 318 confirms the loss is computed against self.gt (clean target).
